text.title: Stage-Appropriate Strategy, Assessment Process, and Agent Workflow
text.summary: Strategy table per stage, assessment process, transition planning, agent pipeline, and critical gotchas for lifecycle classification.

rule.statement: Strategy must match stage. Growth tactics on a declining product and efficiency focus on an early-stage product both waste resources.
rule.rationale: Mismatched strategy is the core failure lifecycle management solves. Growth spend on decline accelerates burn; efficiency focus on Introduction starves learning loops.

list.item: Introduction: MVP, iteration | awareness marketing | lean operations
Growth: features + scale | acquisition marketing | build team and infra
Maturity: optimize + extend | retention marketing | efficiency focus
Decline: maintenance only | migration support | wind down costs

section.title: Assessment Process

list.item: Step 1: Pull 8 quarters of: revenue, growth rate YoY, gross margin, net revenue retention, new logos, churn
Step 2: Compare against category baselines (not framework defaults)
Step 3: Assess stage with confidence band (High/Medium/Low) and alt hypothesis
Step 4: Apply stage-appropriate strategy; identify misaligned current activities
Step 5: Plan transition — what signals next stage, what capabilities are needed

rule.statement: Every stage assessment must include an alt hypothesis: "what would I expect if this assessment is wrong?"
rule.rationale: Single-hypothesis stage calls are overconfident. An alt hypothesis makes the assessment falsifiable and triggers re-evaluation when signals diverge.

section.reference: Metrics table, stage indicator matrix, assessed stage with confidence, actions to start/stop, transition planning.
Per-stage focus areas, KPI targets, investment priorities, risks, transition triggers.

rule.statement: Start transition planning one quarter before the expected stage change. Capability gaps (team mix, infra, KPIs) take a quarter to fill.
rule.rationale: Stage transitions that catch teams unprepared result in wrong-stage activity continuing for months past the transition point, wasting resources.

antipattern.reason: Decline is emotionally loaded and agents soften it. Force the literal stage label in the prompt schema and validate the output field against an allowlist.

antipattern.description: Declaring "back to Growth" from one quarter of re-acceleration after a PLG or AI feature drop.
antipattern.reason: Single-quarter re-acceleration could be a blip. Require 2+ sustained quarters before upgrading a stage assessment.

antipattern.description: Conflating company stage with product stage in a multi-product company.
antipattern.reason: Individual products within a growing company can be in Decline. Specify product scope explicitly in every assessment prompt.

rule.statement: Stage transition decisions (kill, sunset, double-down) are leadership decisions. Agent output is decision-support only; it never auto-publishes.
rule.rationale: Stage transition triggers resource reallocation, team changes, and customer communications. These have organizational authority requirements agents do not satisfy.
